skill.name: amazon-competitor-analyzer
skill.description: Collects Amazon product data for user-provided ASINs through the BrowserAct automation API and creates competitive analysis reports for pricing, reviews, specifications, and positioning.
skill.summary: Analyze Amazon competitors from ASINs with BrowserAct-powered product data collection and report generation.
skill.author: browser-act

security_audit.summary: Static analysis correctly identified network access, environment variable use, and filesystem writes, but the reviewed intent is a BrowserAct API client that writes local reports. No prompt injection, hidden command execution, credential exfiltration, or confirmed malicious behavior was found, but publication should warn users about third-party API-key handling and local file output.

medium_findings.title: Third-Party API Key and Product Data Transmission
medium_findings.description: TRUE POSITIVE: The skill reads BROWSERACT_API_KEY from the environment or CLI and sends requests with an Authorization header to the BrowserAct API. This is expected for the skill, but users must trust BrowserAct with API credentials and submitted ASIN workflow data.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The network calls and Authorization header are direct evidence. The endpoint matches the documented BrowserAct service, so this is an elevated legitimate integration risk rather than confirmed exfiltration.

medium_findings.title: Local Report Writes to User-Selected Output Directory
medium_findings.description: TRUE POSITIVE: The script creates an output directory and writes CSV, Markdown, and JSON reports. This is normal report generation, but a user-supplied output path can overwrite files named amazon_analysis in the selected directory.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file writes and directory creation are explicit. The risk is bounded to local files selected by the user and no evidence shows stealthy persistence or arbitrary file reads.

low_findings.title: Environment File Loading Is Documented Credential Configuration
low_findings.description: TRUE POSITIVE WITH LOW IMPACT: The code loads a local .env file and reads BROWSERACT_API_KEY. This stores and uses a secret, but the behavior is documented and limited to the skill directory and environment configuration.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The .env behavior and API-key requirement are visible in code and documentation. I found no evidence that the skill reads unrelated environment variables or dumps secrets.

low_findings.title: Markdown Shell Commands Are Usage Examples
low_findings.description: FALSE POSITIVE: The external command findings are fenced documentation examples for copying .env, exporting a variable, installing packages, and running the Python script. They are not executed by SKILL.md itself.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The suspicious lines are inside Markdown command examples. No evidence found in SKILL.md that these commands execute automatically.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography and Reconnaissance Findings Are False Positives
low_findings.description: FALSE POSITIVE: The weak cryptography and reconnaissance alerts appear to match words in documentation, argparse text, Markdown headings, or status handling. No hashing, encryption, system inventory, or host reconnaissance code was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited code around argparse and documentation does not implement cryptography or reconnaissance. The static labels are not supported by semantic context.

low_findings.title: Dangerous Combination Heuristic Not Confirmed
low_findings.description: FALSE POSITIVE: The skill combines network calls, credential configuration, and file output, but these capabilities serve the advertised BrowserAct analysis workflow. No evidence found of hidden code execution, obfuscation, prompt injection, or credential theft.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The capability combination is real, but the reviewed code paths are direct and documented. The absence of obfuscation or hidden endpoints lowers confidence in a malicious interpretation.

content.value_statement: Amazon competitor research is slow when teams collect product metrics by hand. This skill automates ASIN-based data collection through BrowserAct and creates comparison reports for faster market analysis.
actual_capabilities: Validates and extracts Amazon ASINs from user input.
Submits ASIN scraping tasks to the BrowserAct workflow API.
Polls BrowserAct task status and retrieves completed product results.
Compares product pricing, ratings, review counts, brands, and specifications.
Generates CSV, Markdown, and JSON reports in a chosen output directory.
Supports single-product and multi-product competitor comparisons.
limitations: Requires a BrowserAct account and BROWSERACT_API_KEY.
Depends on BrowserAct workflow availability and Amazon page accessibility.
Does not independently verify scraped product data accuracy.
May be affected by rate limits, regional availability, and marketplace changes.

use_cases.title: Benchmark Product Listings
use_cases.description: Compare competing Amazon listings by price, rating, review volume, and key specifications before changing a product page.

use_cases.title: Prepare Launch Research
use_cases.description: Collect competitor signals from selected ASINs to identify positioning gaps and likely objections before launch.

use_cases.title: Monitor Category Movement
use_cases.description: Run repeat comparisons for a focused ASIN set and save structured reports for internal review.

prompt_templates.title: Analyze One ASIN
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze this Amazon ASIN with the Amazon competitor analyzer: B09G9GB4MG. Summarize the product data and any obvious competitive signals.

prompt_templates.title: Compare Similar Products
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the Amazon competitor analyzer for these ASINs: B09G9GB4MG, B07ABC11111, and B08N5WRWNW. Compare pricing, reviews, specifications, and brand positioning.

prompt_templates.title: Create Exported Reports
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze these ASINs and save the CSV, Markdown, and JSON reports in ./competitive-output. Highlight price leaders and review leaders after the run.

prompt_templates.title: Find Strategic Openings
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze this competitor ASIN set, then identify pricing gaps, review weaknesses, specification advantages, and possible positioning opportunities for a new listing.

output_examples.input: Three ASINs from the same product category.
output_examples.output: A comparison summary showing the lowest-priced product, highest-rated product, review-volume leader, and notable specification differences.

output_examples.input: A competitor ASIN set with an output directory.
A launch research request for five competitor ASINs.
output_examples.output: A concise market readout with pricing bands, review strength, brand leaders, and practical listing opportunities.

best_practices: Use ASINs from the same category or buying journey for useful comparisons.
Store the BrowserAct API key in a local environment variable or protected .env file.
Review generated reports before making pricing, listing, or inventory decisions.
anti_patterns: Do not use unrelated ASINs and treat the result as one market segment.
Do not publish raw scraped data without checking marketplace rules and data rights.
Do not pass API keys in shared prompts, tickets, logs, or screenshots.

faq.question: Does this skill require an API key?
faq.answer: Yes. It requires BROWSERACT_API_KEY for BrowserAct workflow requests.

faq.question: Does it scrape Amazon directly from the local machine?
faq.answer: No. The Python script sends ASIN tasks to the BrowserAct workflow API and retrieves the results.

faq.question: What report formats can it create?
faq.answer: It can create CSV, Markdown, and JSON reports for the analyzed ASINs.

faq.question: Can it analyze multiple ASINs at once?
faq.answer: Yes. It accepts multiple ASINs and adds a delay between product tasks.

faq.question: Is the data guaranteed to be accurate?
faq.answer: No. Results depend on BrowserAct workflow output, Amazon page structure, and current marketplace availability.

faq.question: Is it safe to publish?
faq.answer: It is publishable with a warning because it uses a third-party API, reads an API key, and writes local report files.
